name: airbrowse
description: Browser automation powered by GitHub Copilot's native models

airbrowse.crawl.maxPages.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl

airbrowse.crawl.delayMs.description: Delay between crawl requests in milliseconds

chatParticipants.name: browse
chatParticipants.description: Browser automation - crawl, extract, test web pages

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getPageText
languageModelTools.description: Extract clean readable text from the current page

selector.description: Optional CSS selector to scope extraction

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Extract clean readable text from the current browser page using Readability. Use this when you need to read, summarize, or analyze web page content. Returns the main article text, title, and excerpt.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getPageMarkdown
languageModelTools.description: Convert the current page content to Markdown

selector.description: Optional CSS selector to scope extraction

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Convert the current browser page content to clean Markdown format. Use this when you need structured text output from a web page for summarization, documentation, or file generation.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getPageHTML
languageModelTools.description: Get raw HTML from the current page

selector.description: Optional CSS selector to scope extraction

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the raw HTML source of the current browser page or a specific element. Use this when you need to inspect the DOM structure or extract HTML content.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getPageTables
languageModelTools.description: Extract all tables from the current page as JSON

selector.description: Optional CSS selector to target a specific table

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Extract all HTML tables from the current browser page as structured JSON arrays with headers and rows. Use this when the user wants to extract tabular data for export to Excel, CSV, or analysis.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getPageLinks
languageModelTools.description: Get all links from the current page

selector.description: Optional CSS selector to scope link extraction

sameDomain.description: If true, only return links on the same domain

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get all hyperlinks from the current browser page with their text, URL, and whether they are internal links. Use this to discover pages for crawling or to analyze site structure.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getPageStructure
languageModelTools.description: Get the heading outline (h1-h6) of the current page
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the heading outline (h1-h6 hierarchy) of the current browser page. Use this to understand page structure before extracting specific sections.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_screenshot
languageModelTools.description: Capture the current page as a PNG image

fullPage.description: If true, capture the full scrollable page

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Capture a screenshot of the current browser page as a PNG image. Use this for visual verification during UI testing or to show the user what the page looks like.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_navigateTo
languageModelTools.description: Navigate the browser to a specified URL
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Navigate the browser to a specified URL. Use this to go to a new page before reading or interacting with it.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_navigateBack
languageModelTools.description: Go back in browser history
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Go back to the previous page in browser history. Use this to return to a prior page during multi-page workflows.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getCurrentUrl
languageModelTools.description: Get the current URL and page title
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the current URL and page title from the browser. Use this to verify which page is currently loaded.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_click
languageModelTools.description: Click an element on the page

selector.description: CSS selector of the element to click

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Click an element on the current browser page identified by CSS selector. Use this for UI interaction like clicking buttons, links, or other interactive elements.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_type
languageModelTools.description: Type text into an input element

selector.description: CSS selector of the input element

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Type text into an input field on the current browser page. Use this to fill in forms, search boxes, or other text inputs.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_selectOption
languageModelTools.description: Select an option from a dropdown element

selector.description: CSS selector of the select element

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Select an option from a dropdown/select element on the current browser page. Use this to choose values from dropdown menus.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_submitForm
languageModelTools.description: Submit a form element

selector.description: CSS selector of the form element

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Submit a form on the current browser page. Use this after filling in form fields to submit the form.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_scrollTo
languageModelTools.description: Scroll the page to an element or position

selector.description: Optional CSS selector to scroll to

y.description: Optional vertical scroll position in pixels

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Scroll the browser page to a specific element or vertical position. Use this to bring content into view before interacting with it.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_crawlSite
languageModelTools.description: Crawl multiple pages starting from a URL

startUrl.description: The URL to start crawling from

maxPages.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Crawl multiple pages on the same domain starting from a URL. Extracts text, markdown, tables, and links from each page. Use this when the user wants to scrape or summarize an entire site or section.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_crawlCancel
languageModelTools.description: Cancel a running crawl operation
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Cancel a currently running crawl operation. Use this to stop a crawl that is taking too long or is no longer needed.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getConsoleLogs
languageModelTools.description: Get console output from the browser
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get console log output from the browser (log, warn, error). Use this for debugging or monitoring browser-side issues.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_getNetworkRequests
languageModelTools.description: Get network requests made by the page

filter.description: Optional filter string to match request URLs

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get recent network requests made by the browser page. Use this for debugging API calls or monitoring page resource loading.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_generateExcel
languageModelTools.description: Create an Excel (.xlsx) file from data

sheets.description: Array of sheet definitions with name, headers, and rows

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create an Excel (.xlsx) file from structured data with multiple sheets, headers, and rows. Use this when the user wants to export data to a spreadsheet.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_generateCSV
languageModelTools.description: Create a CSV file from data
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create a CSV file from structured data with headers and rows. Use this when the user wants a simple text-based data export.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_generateWord
languageModelTools.description: Create a Word (.docx) document
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create a Word (.docx) document with a title and sections. Use this when the user wants to generate a formatted report or document.

languageModelTools.name: airbrowse_generateMarkdown
languageModelTools.description: Create a Markdown (.md) file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create a Markdown (.md) file with a title and content. Use this when the user wants to save content as Markdown.
